articles.headline: Two US Troops Killed in Iran Strikes; Tehran Halts Peace Pact
articles.summary: Two US military personnel died following missile and drone attacks by Iran-backed forces on a base in Jordan. In the aftermath, Iran announced it is suspending its commitments to the international peace pact signed a month ago, citing 'unforgettable lessons' for its adversaries. US forces are evaluating retaliatory options.

articles.headline: SC Judge Criticizes Govt Decisions for Hurting Arbitration Hub Goal
articles.summary: Supreme Court Justice Ujjal Bhuyan warned that 'erratic' court verdicts and 'regressive' decisions by the Finance Ministry are undermining India's goal of becoming a global arbitration hub. He specifically cited a recent ministry memorandum that restricted the use of arbitration in government contracts as a significant policy shift.

articles.headline: Delhi Eases Land Rules for Setting Up Private Schools
articles.summary: The Delhi government has relaxed land requirement norms for opening new private schools in high-density areas. The move aims to streamline administrative processes and increase educational facilities under the Right to Education Act. Minimum land requirements have been reduced to improve school accessibility in the capital.

articles.headline: Nirmohi Akhara Petitions Supreme Court for Ram Temple Trust Rejig
articles.summary: The Nirmohi Akhara has moved the Supreme Court seeking a modification of the 2019 Ayodhya judgment. The petition requests 'appropriate representation' for the Akhara in the Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. They allege mismanagement and lack of transparency in the current trust’s functioning.

articles.headline: Telangana Orders SIT Probe Into Dharani Portal Land Irregularities
articles.summary: The Telangana government has constituted a Special Investigation Team (SIT) to probe alleged irregularities in the Dharani land portal. The audit identified suspicious transactions and unauthorized changes in land records affecting over 10,000 files. The SIT will examine potential data breaches and illegal land mutations during the previous regime.

articles.headline: Former Prime Minister Deve Gowda’s Wife Chennamma Passes Away
articles.summary: Chennamma Deve Gowda, wife of former Prime Minister H.D. Deve Gowda, passed away at the age of 89 due to a cardiac arrest in Bengaluru. She was undergoing treatment for a respiratory ailment. Political leaders across the spectrum expressed their condolences, and she will be accorded state honors for her funeral.

articles.headline: CM Shivakumar Calls Emergency Meeting Over Karnataka Drought Crisis
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar has summoned an emergency cabinet meeting to address the worsening drought in the state. He directed officials to prioritize drinking water and avoid releasing dam water for agriculture until monsoons stabilize. The state will seek a central team from the Union government to assess crop damage.

articles.headline: Five Villagers Drown During Fishing Trip in Godavari River
articles.summary: Five tribal villagers, including three women, drowned in the Godavari River in Andhra Pradesh's Polavaram district. The group was swept away by a sudden surge in water while fishing. Rescue teams have recovered all five bodies. Police registered a case of accidental death and are investigating the circumstances.

articles.headline: DMK Moves Adjournment Motions in Parliament Over Mekedatu Project
articles.summary: The DMK has submitted adjournment motions in both houses of Parliament regarding the Mekedatu dam project. The party is protesting Karnataka's plan to build the dam on the Cauvery River, citing potential water shortages for Tamil Nadu farmers. The DMK is demanding the Centre's intervention to resolve the long-standing dispute.

articles.headline: TDP Leader Arrested for Assaulting Woman in Guntur
articles.summary: Police in Andhra Pradesh arrested a TDP leader and eight others for allegedly stripping and publicly assaulting a woman in Guntur. The incident reportedly followed a dispute over installing a motor pump for a water pipeline. The TDP has suspended the leader pending further investigation into the violent encounter.

articles.headline: Kerala Power Consumption Reaches 10-Year High During Crunch
articles.summary: Kerala recorded its highest daily power consumption in a decade, reaching 102 million units due to high temperatures. The state electricity board has warned of potential supply cuts during peak evening hours as reservoir levels remain low. The board has been authorized to purchase 200 MW of power at ₹5.96 per unit to meet the shortfall.

articles.headline: Telangana to Enact Strict Law Against Food Adulteration
articles.summary: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy announced plans for a stringent law to curb food adulteration in the state. The proposed legislation includes heavy penalties and fast-track trials for those found guilty of using harmful chemicals in fruits, vegetables, and milk. A task force will be formed to conduct regular inspections across the state.

articles.headline: Bill on Higher Education Regulator Likely to Face Delays
articles.summary: The Union government may delay the bill to establish a new higher education regulator following protests from various stakeholders and the opposition. The proposed legislation, which aims to replace the UGC, has been referred to a joint parliamentary committee for further review. Critics argue the bill may erode the autonomy of universities.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Rules F-Word Offensive But Not Obscene
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that while the use of the 'F-word' may be offensive and vulgar, it does not necessarily constitute obscenity under Section 294(b) of the IPC. The bench noted that the context and audience determine if the language is 'obscene' or merely 'abusive'. The ruling came during a case involving a dispute between neighbors.

articles.headline: Delhi Fire Service to Conduct Surprise Preparedness Checks
articles.summary: The Delhi Fire Service (DFS) will launch surprise inspections and response-time drills at fire stations across the city. This initiative follows multiple major fire incidents in the capital. The checks will evaluate the readiness of firefighting equipment, personnel training, and communication systems to ensure faster response times.

articles.headline: Delhi Police Prohibit CJP Parliament March Citing Security
articles.summary: Delhi Police have denied permission for a planned march to Parliament by the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP). Authorities cited security concerns and potential disruption to public order in the high-security zone. Security has been beefed up at Jantar Mantar and border areas to prevent protesters from gathering near the complex.

articles.headline: Businessman Shot Dead by Motorcycle-Borne Assailants in Bawana
articles.summary: A 45-year-old businessman was shot dead in Delhi's Bawana area by three unidentified attackers on a motorcycle. The victim was targeted near his home in broad daylight. Police suspect a link to a fugitive gangster or personal rivalry. A murder case has been registered, and multiple teams are scanning CCTV footage for leads.

articles.headline: Canada to Deport Man Linked to Lawrence Bishnoi Gang
articles.summary: Canada to deport individual with alleged links to the Lawrence Bishnoi criminal gang after intelligence boards found grounds of his involvement in gang activities. The order comes amid a spike in extortion-related shootings in Calgary and follows an investigation into organized crime in the region.

articles.headline: MP Bans Overseas Trips for Officials to Cut Spending
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh government has banned overseas official trips to curb administrative expenditure. Exceptions will only be made for essential cases requiring high-level approval. The move is part of a broader austerity drive aimed at managing the state's budget deficit. Officials have also been directed to use domestic airlines for travel.

articles.headline: Azam Khan’s Two-Year Prison Sentence Upheld in Hate Speech
articles.summary: A special court in Meerut has upheld the two-year prison sentence of Samajwadi Party leader Azam Khan in a 2019 hate speech case. The court dismissed Khan's appeal against the lower court's verdict. The conviction had led to his disqualification from the UP Legislative Assembly. Khan remains in custody as he faces multiple other cases.

articles.headline: NIA Arrests Two for Killing Six Nagas in Manipur
articles.summary: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has arrested two individuals in connection with the killing of six Nagas in Manipur's Senapati district. The suspects are allegedly linked to an insurgent group. The killings occurred during a period of heightened ethnic tension in the state. The NIA is continuing its probe to identify others involved.

articles.headline: Priest at Ancient UP Temple Beaten to Death
articles.summary: A priest at a historic Hanuman temple in Uttar Pradesh's Mahoba district was found beaten to death. The victim, identified as Udit Narayan Mishra, was attacked with sharp objects inside the temple premises. Police have registered a murder case and are investigating potential motives, including robbery or personal enmity.

articles.headline: India Face England in Decisive Third ODI at Lord’s
articles.summary: The Indian cricket team, led by Rohit Sharma, faces England in the third and final ODI at Lord’s. The series is currently tied at 1-1. All eyes are on the openers and the bowling unit to clinch the series on English soil. This match is seen as crucial for India's preparation for the upcoming Champions Trophy.

articles.headline: Nvidia CEO’s Signature Leather Jacket Sells for Nearly $1M
articles.summary: A black leather jacket previously worn by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was sold at a charity auction for nearly $1 million. The jacket, known for being Huang's signature attire at tech launches, was purchased by an anonymous bidder. The proceeds will go towards supporting computer science education programs in underprivileged areas.

articles.headline: Ukraine Drone Strikes Hit Multiple Strategic Sites Across Russia
articles.summary: Ukrainian forces launched a series of drone strikes targeting military and industrial sites across several Russian regions. Reports indicate damage to oil depots and warehouses. This escalation follows recent Russian missile attacks on Ukrainian infrastructure. The Russian defense ministry claimed to have intercepted most of the drones.

articles.headline: US Ends State of Emergency for Hong Kong After Beijing Move
articles.summary: The United States has officially ended the state of emergency regarding Hong Kong that was declared in 2020. The decision follows a review of Beijing's security policies in the territory. While some sanctions remain, the move signals a slight shift in US policy as it monitors the implementation of international treaties in the region.

articles.headline: Private bank investors missed out on PSU-led valuation U-turn
articles.summary: Analysis reveals that private lenders like HDFC and Axis have lagged behind PSU peers since 2019 despite strong growth. While PSU banks rallied significantly, valuation multiples for major private banks have halved, potentially offering entry points for investors as earnings trajectories remain solid in the high-growth Indian economy.

articles.headline: PNB Q1 net profit trebles on lower tax and cost control
articles.summary: Punjab National Bank's net profit surged to ₹3,252 crore in Q1 FY25, a 213% year-on-year increase. The sharp growth was driven by a significant reduction in tax outgo and effective cost management. Net interest income grew by 10.2% to ₹10,476 crore during the quarter.

articles.headline: Tech Call: Godrej Properties remains bullish for long-term investors
articles.summary: Technical analysis suggests a positive outlook for Godrej Properties shares. Following a correction from April highs, the stock shows strong support at ₹1,850. Analysts suggest accumulating at current levels with targets ranging from ₹2,710 to ₹3,450 for long-term gains based on structural trends.

articles.headline: Global oil reserves thin out as Hormuz disruption risks loom
articles.summary: Global oil markets face vulnerability as strategic petroleum reserves and commercial inventories decrease. Recent geopolitical tensions have spiked Brent prices, and analysts warn that further disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz could push prices toward $115 per barrel as the market buffer cushion weakens.

articles.headline: IFSCA proposal may allow direct listing on GIFT City exchanges
articles.summary: The International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA) has proposed a framework for companies to list directly on IFSC exchanges without an initial public offering. The move aims to provide visibility and liquidity for startups and established firms while bypassing traditional IPO complexities and costs.

articles.headline: AI jitters drive global semiconductor stocks toward a bear market
articles.summary: Concerns over the timeline for AI-related returns and geopolitical tensions have triggered a sell-off in semiconductor stocks. The Nasdaq 100 fell 1.5% as investors questioned whether the massive infrastructure spending by tech giants on artificial intelligence will yield immediate profitable results.

articles.headline: New Income Tax Return forms show widening gap in complexity
articles.summary: A comparison of ITR-1 (Sahaj) and ITR-2 forms for the latest assessment year reveals diverging levels of detail. While ITR-1 remains streamlined for simple income sources, ITR-2 has become more complex, requiring extensive disclosures on capital gains, foreign assets, and virtual digital assets.

articles.headline: Building a hybrid portfolio for low to moderate risk appetite
articles.summary: Analysis of the ICICI Prudential Regular Savings Fund highlights the advantages of hybrid investing. By balancing equity (15-25%) with high-quality debt, the fund aims for superior risk-adjusted returns over the long term, focusing on large-cap stocks and stable income from debt instruments.

articles.headline: bl.portfolio Star Track Mutual Fund Ratings updated for June 2024
articles.summary: The latest mutual fund ratings evaluated 488 schemes across 32 categories based on rolling returns and risk-adjusted metrics. Key upgrades include Nippon India Large Cap and HDFC Flexi Cap, while several schemes faced downgrades due to shifting performance cycles and volatile market conditions.

articles.headline: Mutual Fund transmission process further streamlined for ease of claims
articles.summary: The Association of Mutual Funds in India (AMFI) has simplified the process for claimants to access units or proceeds after the death of a holder. New guidelines address common issues like name mismatches and minor/major status transitions to reduce friction for legal heirs and nominees.

articles.headline: Cube Highways InvIT IPO offers steady yields amid valuation risks
articles.summary: Cube Highways Trust is launching a ₹5,000-crore public issue with a price band of ₹151-152. While the InvIT offers exposure to a portfolio of 27 road assets and an attractive distribution yield of approximately 9%, investors are advised to weigh interest rate risks and current valuation premiums.

articles.headline: LG Electronics eyes growth in consumer durables after muted Q1
articles.summary: Despite a muted performance in early 2024 due to weather and geopolitical factors, LG Electronics India remains optimistic. The company plans to double capacity at its Sri City plant and expand its Essential series to capture more market share in the home appliances segment.

articles.headline: Global boardroom chatter highlights India as a primary growth engine
articles.summary: Recent earnings calls from global giants like Intuitive Surgical and Jabil reveal a strong focus on the Indian market. Companies are expanding their manufacturing and R&D footprints in the country, citing the 'Make in India' initiative and robust domestic demand as key drivers for investment.

articles.headline: Short covering by FIIs lifts Nifty 50 above key levels
articles.summary: The Nifty 50 and Nifty Bank showed resilience, ending the week higher as Foreign Institutional Investors reduced their net short positions. Technical indicators suggest a bullish bias in the short term as long as the index maintains its support above the 24,300 level.

articles.headline: Blueprint for successful redevelopment of urban housing societies
articles.summary: As urban housing stock in India ages, redevelopment is emerging as a critical trend. Apartment owners must navigate legal, financial, and technical challenges, including securing 70% member consent and conducting thorough due diligence on developers to ensure project viability.

articles.headline: PFRDA moves to strengthen accessibility standards for pension services
articles.summary: The Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) has instructed intermediaries to upgrade their infrastructure and digital platforms. The initiative aims to ensure seamless access for all citizens and full compliance with statutory accessibility provisions for pension accounts.

articles.headline: Multiplexes and eateries prepare for massive FIFA World Cup surge
articles.summary: Businesses across India are lacing up for a late-night revenue boost during the FIFA World Cup finale. Multiplexes are screening the match live, while restaurants in major cities have obtained special permissions to stay open late to accommodate football fans and celebration crowds.

articles.headline: Vikram-1 takes India's private space sector to new orbit
articles.summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1, marking a significant milestone for India's private space industry. The seven-story rocket placed multiple satellites into Low Earth Orbit from Sriharikota, demonstrating the technical capability of indigenous private launch vehicles.

articles.headline: Axis, Kotak, and IDBI report strong Q1 profits on recoveries
articles.summary: Major private lenders reported healthy profit growth in the June quarter. Axis Bank's profit rose 22.5% to ₹7,114 crore, while Kotak Mahindra Bank saw a 26% increase. YES Bank also posted strong results, supported by improved asset quality and higher recoveries from bad debts.

articles.headline: Opposition's resolve in Parliament won't change: K.C. Venugopal
articles.summary: Congress general secretary K.C. Venugopal stated the INDIA bloc will oppose any attempt to alter constitutional balance and undermine federalism, particularly regarding the delimitation bill. He said a change in seating won't change their resolve and demanded an all-party meeting.

articles.headline: What are the gains from the India-U.K. trade deal?
articles.summary: This FAQ explores the benefits of the India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), which came into effect on July 15. It highlights immediate tariff removals by the U.K. on 96.8% of lines and for India on 30.3% of goods, worker benefits through the Double Contribution Convention, and areas excluded like an investment agreement.

articles.headline: Delhi's first women's police station faces real test after month
articles.summary: A month after its inauguration, Delhi's first women's police station at Sabzi Mandi has received 154 complaints and filed nine FIRs. While welcomed, challenges include delays, coordination issues, and a need for more trained counsellors and staff to handle the volume of women-related cases.

articles.headline: Delhi to get 2,800 new e-buses by 2028 under PM e-Drive
articles.summary: Delhi will add 2,800 new air-conditioned low-floor electric buses to its public transport fleet by August 2028 under Phase I of the PM e-Drive Scheme, aiming to improve air quality and last-mile connectivity. This will boost the fleet to 14,000 by 2028-29.

articles.headline: 79,404 students accept DU UG seats in first admission round
articles.summary: 79,404 candidates accepted undergraduate seats allotted in the University of Delhi's first admission round. The university made 93,033 allotments for 71,600 seats. Fee payment deadline is July 21; the second allocation list releases July 25.

articles.headline: Parents' body seeks audit of private schools, fee regulation
articles.summary: The newly launched Parents Association for Welfare and Justice (PAWJ) demanded an independent financial audit of private unaided schools and the constitution of School Level Fee Regulation Committees to ensure fee transparency.

articles.headline: Delhi Police Commissioner calls on L-G after taking charge
articles.summary: Newly appointed Delhi Police Commissioner Anurag Kumar met Lieutenant-Governor Taranjit Singh Sandhu at Lok Niwas on Saturday, after taking charge on Friday.

articles.headline: KEM Hospital canteen's licence suspended over unhygienic conditions
articles.summary: Maharashtra's Food and Drug Administration suspended the licence of a canteen at Mumbai's KEM Hospital after an inspection found unhygienic conditions and food safety violations. Four food samples were collected for analysis.

articles.headline: PM silent on farmers' issues, Punjab visit 'flop show': AAP
articles.summary: AAP accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of failing to address Punjab's key issues, including farmer deaths, MSP legal guarantee, and withholding State funds, during his visit. AAP claimed the BJP had made "baseless allegations" against the State government.

articles.headline: Activists demand safeguards for vulnerable after Odisha pension death
articles.summary: Activists demanded a robust mechanism to protect vulnerable sections after a 67-year-old widow allegedly died by suicide following the stoppage of her government pension in Odisha. The case highlighted systemic issues and delays affecting 20 lakh pension holders.

articles.headline: NCP(SP) not joining NDA or merging with Congress: Sule
articles.summary: Nationalist Congress Party (Sharadchandra Pawar) working president Supriya Sule dismissed speculation about her party joining the NDA or merging with the ruling NCP, stating no political party had contacted them with such proposals. She affirmed loyalty to Sharad Pawar.

articles.headline: Hunger strike against Ken-Betwa Link Project enters 15th day
articles.summary: An indefinite hunger strike against the Ken-Betwa Link Project in Bundelkhand, Madhya Pradesh, entered its 15th day. Protesters, mainly tribal women, allege inadequate rehabilitation and grievances related to displacement, engaging in various forms of 'satyagraha'.

articles.headline: U.P. CM meets slain Dalit student's kin, assures action
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath met the family of murdered Dalit student Lalita Gautam in Meerut, assuring them of government support and strict action against the culprits. He instructed police for effective case pursuit.

articles.headline: Targeting Muslim structures to increase ahead of U.P. election: Oppn.
articles.summary: Opposition parties in Uttar Pradesh expressed apprehension that targeting Muslim-linked structures by the ruling BJP could intensify for "political gains" ahead of next year's Assembly election, citing the demolition notice to Mohammad Ali Jauhar University.

articles.headline: Doctors' assault: HC stays bail to corporator, orders surrender
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court stayed the bail granted by a lower court to Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, accused of assaulting doctors at a municipal hospital in Thane district, calling the lower court's decision "perverse" and noting his criminal antecedents.

articles.headline: Ayodhya Ram temple donation theft creates trust crisis
articles.summary: Allegations of donation theft at Ayodhya's Ram temple have triggered a crisis of trust among pilgrims and residents. While faith in Lord Ram remains, many devotees are now cautious about donating. Eight persons are accused, and ₹80 lakh cash seized, impacting local tourism.

articles.headline: Delimitation Bill a matter of national concern: K.C. Venugopal
articles.summary: Congress general secretary K.C. Venugopal stated the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill on delimitation is a national concern, not just a party issue, as it threatens India's constitutional framework. He opposed attempts to alter federalism and democratic representation and demanded an all-party meeting.

articles.headline: Panels on Ministers' removal, VBSA Bills defer meetings
articles.summary: Joint Parliamentary Committees examining the Constitution Amendment Bill on Ministers' removal and the Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhishthan (VBSA) Bill cancelled or deferred meetings to adopt draft reports. Opposition leaders called it a "big victory" following previous government defeats.

articles.headline: Two more arrested in Manipur over killing of six Nagas
articles.summary: A joint team arrested Lungoulal Vaiphei and Lunminthang Sitlhou in Manipur in connection with the abduction and killing of six Liangmai Naga civilians. This marks the second set of arrests in the case, which the NIA is handling, with further arrests anticipated.

articles.headline: 21 protesters continue Jantar Mantar fast despite Wangchuk's removal
articles.summary: Following activist Sonam Wangchuk's removal to hospital, at least 21 protesters from the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) continued their hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, demanding the Union Education Minister's resignation. Doctors advised rest, but protesters remain resolute, citing deteriorated health conditions.

articles.headline: AIIMS expert backed urgent treatment for Sonam Wangchuk
articles.summary: Safdarjung Hospital reported an AIIMS expert backed urgent medical intervention for Sonam Wangchuk due to dehydration, despite him being conscious and stable. Wangchuk refused intravenous fluids and medications, and his wife requested discharge to a facility of her choice.

articles.headline: NC expels Akhoon amid Kargil CEC rotational agreement row
articles.summary: The National Conference (NC) expelled Dr. Mohammad Jaffer Akhoon for six years due to "anti-party activities" after he refused to step down as Chief Executive Councillor of LAHDC-Kargil, violating a rotational agreement with ally Congress.

articles.headline: Invasive plants turn hosts for butterflies in Assam park
articles.summary: A Bodoland University study in Raimona National Park found that invasive plant species like Lantana camara and Chromolaena odorata, often excluded from forest management, serve as major nectar and larval host plants for butterflies, highlighting their unexpected ecological role.

articles.headline: Mumbai Police probing threat against Aamir Khan from Bishnoi associates
articles.summary: Mumbai Police are investigating a threat issued against Bollywood actor Aamir Khan, purportedly from associates of jailed gangster Lawrence Bishnoi. The threat, circulated via social media, accused Khan of promoting 'love jihad'. No formal complaint has been lodged.

articles.headline: Five injured in blast at Bengal sponge iron factory
articles.summary: A furnace exploded at a sponge iron factory in Paschim Bardhaman district, West Bengal, injuring at least five workers. The blast, audible from a kilometre away, caused a fire, making rescue operations difficult.

articles.headline: BCI lays down social media norms for advocates, law students
articles.summary: The Bar Council of India (BCI) issued strict social media guidelines, prohibiting advocates and law students from creating content that sensationalises court proceedings or ridicules judges. It also bans AI-generated images, deepfake videos, and undisclosed AI legal content.

articles.headline: Couple, three children found dead in Raipur house
articles.summary: A couple and their three minor children were found dead in their rented house in Raipur, Chhattisgarh. Preliminary investigations suggest the man poisoned his family before hanging himself; forensic analysis will confirm the cause of death.

articles.headline: Vaishno Devi, Amarnath yatras paused for safety reviews
articles.summary: Authorities in Jammu and Kashmir temporarily suspended the Amarnath and Vaishno Devi yatras from July 19 due to an inclement weather forecast issued by the IMD, citing precautionary measures to ensure pilgrim safety.

articles.headline: NMC limits routine faculty eligibility reviews for medical colleges
articles.summary: The National Medical Commission (NMC) will no longer process routine requests from faculty and medical colleges for eligibility reviews for teaching positions, shifting responsibility primarily to the appointing authority. More complicated cases require a ₹25,000 fee.

articles.headline: PNB Q1 profit surges threefold to ₹5,253 crore
articles.summary: Punjab National Bank (PNB) reported over a threefold jump in standalone net profit to ₹5,253 crore for the quarter ended June 30, mainly due to a ₹3,358 crore reduction in tax payment from migrating to a new tax regime.

articles.headline: Promoters earned less from listing firms in CY26: Report
articles.summary: Promoters and venture capitalists earned a lower share (84%) of total offer for sale (OFS) proceeds from stock market listings in H1 2026 (₹10,696 crore) compared to 95.5% in H1 2025. This indicates a potential shift in capital deployment.

articles.headline: India Inc. CSR spend up 17% to ₹40,794 cr. in FY25
articles.summary: Corporate India's CSR spending rose 17% year-on-year to ₹40,794 crore in FY25, with cumulative investment reaching ₹2.61 lakh crore over a decade. The report by Fulcrum showed 72,233 projects, with Reliance Industries Ltd. as the highest spender.

articles.headline: Gujarat tops NITI Aayog's Investment Friendliness Index 2026
articles.summary: Gujarat emerged as India’s leading investment destination, topping NITI Aayog’s first-ever Investment Friendliness Index 2026 with 56.6 points, ahead of Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu. The index assessed 17 major states across 84 indicators.

articles.headline: Skyroot faces steep climb to prove Vikram-1 commercial viability
articles.summary: Following the successful orbital launch of Vikram-1, Skyroot Aerospace faces challenges in proving commercial viability in a competitive, evolving small-satellite launch market. The NEWS ANALYSIS highlights low-margin services, competition from rideshares and ISRO, and the need for international customers to sustain growth.

articles.headline: Power grids face unexpected slow-burn threat from solar storms
articles.summary: A new study in Space Weather reveals solar storms pose an unexpected "slow-burn" threat to power grids, which existing tools may not fully capture. It cites a 2015 geomagnetic storm in New Zealand that induced prolonged, low-level currents, potentially causing transformer overheating.

articles.headline: Surprise found in why some ancient brains don’t decay
articles.summary: A new study found that in wet, low-oxygen environments, certain brain proteins bind into stable structures that resist decay, explaining the preservation of human brains in archaeological sites. These proteins are also involved in brain ageing and Alzheimer's.

articles.headline: Non-animal testing key to next phase of pharma growth
articles.summary: Adopting non-animal testing models (NAMs) is crucial for India's pharmaceutical growth, enhancing drug discovery, reducing attrition, and accelerating development. Despite existing capabilities, a unified national strategy and regulatory clarity are needed to integrate NAMs and improve global competitiveness.

articles.headline: New monkey species found in Central Africa: Colobus congoensis
articles.summary: A new species of African monkey, Colobus congoensis or 'Likweli', was confirmed in Lomami National Park, Central Africa. Small and black, it is distinguished by an orange-cream patch around its mouth and a white tail patch. Proposed as 'endangered'.

articles.headline: Imbalance breaks open soggy physics puzzle: reverse sprinkler
articles.summary: Scientists found that a reverse lawn sprinkler, sucking fluid in, rotates opposite to a regular sprinkler. This is due to centrifugal forces creating slight asymmetry in water flow through curved arms, generating angular momentum.

articles.headline: Knowledge work in the age of agentic AI
articles.summary: This article discusses how agentic AI systems, capable of pursuing goals and sequencing decisions without constant human oversight, are transforming knowledge work. It suggests that human value will shift towards defining tasks well and checking AI output, requiring judgment and contextual understanding.

articles.headline: Why does our body scent change distinctly as we age?
articles.summary: The distinct body scent associated with ageing is primarily caused by 2-nonenal, an unsaturated aldehyde. Around 40, the body's antioxidant defenses weaken, allowing fats on the skin to break down and produce this compound, which resists standard soaps.

articles.headline: How does India's newly launched hydrogen-powered train work?
articles.summary: India's first hydrogen fuel cell-powered train operates on an 89-km route in Haryana, generating electricity onboard from hydrogen and oxygen. It emits only water vapour and heat. The article details its technology, refuelling process, safety features, and future plans for heritage railways.

articles.headline: What goals does Semicon 2.0 aim to achieve?
articles.summary: Semicon 2.0, with a ₹1.27 lakh crore outlay, aims to encourage semiconductor fabrication, assembly, and packaging. It expands beyond phase 1 to cover chip design, capital machinery, chemicals, and R&D, targeting 28nm chips to boost domestic electronics manufacturing and reduce import reliance.

articles.headline: Ahmad Vahidi, Iran's commander of asymmetric warfare
articles.summary: Brigadier General Ahmad Vahidi, former IRGC spymaster and Defence Minister, now leads Iran's military response to relentless U.S. bombardment. An architect of Iran’s 'Axis of Resistance' and a proponent of asymmetric warfare, he faces his greatest test amid war and leadership changes.

articles.headline: Tamil political groups launch common platform in Sri Lanka
articles.summary: Prominent political parties and groups representing Sri Lanka’s Tamil-speaking ethnic minorities launched a "common platform" to voice shared concerns. Their specific programme focuses on a new Constitution, Provincial Council elections, and resolving land disputes for war-affected Tamils and Malaiyaha Tamils.

articles.headline: Sonam Wangchuk: face of CJP's protest for NEET accountability
articles.summary: Educationist Sonam Wangchuk, after a 21-day fast, has emerged as the face of the Cockroach Janta Party's protest in Delhi, demanding the Union Education Minister's resignation and accountability for NEET examination blunders. His health is a growing concern.

articles.headline: IRGC says two oil tankers explode after hitting mines in Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards claimed two oil tankers hit mines and exploded in the Strait of Hormuz, a claim disputed by the U.S. military. The IRGC warned sailors against entering the "minefield" for their safety and capital.

articles.headline: Two U.S. troops dead, one missing after Iran hits Jordan base
articles.summary: The U.S. military reported two troop deaths and one missing after an Iranian attack on a base in Jordan on Friday, marking the first direct Iranian fire casualties since the war's opening days. Since the war began, 16 U.S. service members have been killed.

articles.headline: Lebanon's President Aoun departs for Washington to meet Trump
articles.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun left Beirut for Washington to meet Donald Trump, following talks between Lebanon and Israel in Italy. This marks the first visit by a Lebanese head of state to Washington since 2009.

articles.headline: Hezbollah holds mass funeral for fighters in south Lebanon
articles.summary: Hezbollah held a mass funeral in Majdal Selm, south Lebanon, for dozens of people, primarily fighters killed in recent clashes with Israel. They buried 44 individuals, including 39 fighters and four civilians.

articles.headline: Iran 'suspends commitments' to interim agreement with U.S.
articles.summary: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister stated Tehran suspended its commitments to an interim deal with the U.S., citing U.S. violations. This came as U.S. and Iran exchanged strikes, with significant damage reported in Kuwait from Iranian strikes on a desalination plant and oil facility.

articles.headline: Nine killed in Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions
articles.summary: Nine people were killed and over 80 wounded in Russia by Ukrainian drone attacks overnight and Saturday. Warehouses of online retailer Wildberries were hit, along with an oil depot in Noginsk, causing fires and evacuations.

articles.headline: Venezuela quake toll crosses 5,000; IMF supports relief efforts
articles.summary: Twin earthquakes in Venezuela last month killed over 5,000 people, primarily in La Guaira. Interim President Delcy Rodriguez announced the country tapped $346 million from the IMF for reconstruction efforts, with 16,740 people injured.

articles.headline: New York Mayor Mamdani mulling Netanyahu's arrest during UN meet
articles.summary: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani is considering whether to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during an upcoming UN summit, calling him a "war criminal." Netanyahu's camp sharply rebuked Mamdani, who admitted uncertainty about his power to order the arrest.

articles.headline: Cuban Ambassador calls for lifting U.S. energy blockade
articles.summary: Cuban Ambassador to India Juan Carlos Marsan Aguilera called for lifting the U.S. oil blockade against Cuba, stating it caused "unprecedented suffering" and a humanitarian crisis. He cited over 100,000 pending medical surgeries and increased infant mortality due to frequent power cuts.

articles.headline: Spain is a great team, everything worries me: Scaloni
articles.summary: Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni expressed wariness about Spain, calling them a "great team" and saying everything about them worries him ahead of the World Cup final. He hopes for a win but acknowledges the incredible journey regardless.

articles.headline: We will use all our 'weapons' to beat Argentina: de la Fuente
articles.summary: Spain head coach Luis de la Fuente declared his team would use "all our weapons" to win the World Cup final against Argentina. He highlighted his team's adaptability and acknowledged Argentina's strong performance in recent years.

articles.headline: This will be the most physical match: Rodri on title decider
articles.summary: Spain captain Rodri anticipates the World Cup final against Argentina to be the "most physical match." He noted his team's varied playstyle across games and emphasized their readiness to attack, defend, or counter.

articles.headline: I hope Yamal and Spain don't have a good game: Messi
articles.summary: Argentina skipper Lionel Messi expressed admiration for Lamine Yamal but cheekily hoped Yamal and Spain wouldn't perform well in the World Cup final against Argentina. Messi acknowledged Yamal's potential to achieve something historic.

articles.headline: Sobers stood out among the greats, says Michael Holding
articles.summary: Former West Indies fast bowler Michael Holding described the late Garry Sobers as outstanding even among cricket's greats, noting his humility despite impressive Test statistics (8,032 runs, 235 wickets). Holding recounted Ian Chappell calling Sobers "the best cricketer that ever lived."

articles.headline: 'No concerns with the way Rohit is batting': Morkel
articles.summary: India bowling coach Morne Morkel defended Rohit Sharma's form, stating he has "no worries and concerns at all" about the premier batter. Morkel emphasized Rohit's experience and calming influence, expecting him to overcome recent struggles.

articles.headline: Joe Root an ideal role model for England team: Brook
articles.summary: England captain Harry Brook praised Joe Root as an "ideal role model" for the team, highlighting his ability to make batting look easy and rotate strike effectively. Brook anticipated a challenging Lord's pitch requiring strong new-ball performance and strike rotation.

articles.headline: Pogacar wins 14th stage, extends Tour de France lead
articles.summary: Tadej Pogacar won the 14th stage of the Tour de France in the Vosges, extending his overall lead over Jonas Vingegaard to more than four minutes. This was Pogacar's fourth stage win in this edition.

articles.headline: V.S. Rathanvel becomes India's 99th Chess Grandmaster
articles.summary: V.S. Rathanvel became India's 99th Grandmaster after crossing the 2500 Elo mark at the 3rd Guwahati Smart City International Open. The Coimbatore-born player had secured three norms in 2022.

articles.headline: Herbert, Burns tie Major championship record with 62s at Open
articles.summary: Lucas Herbert and Sam Burns tied the Major championship record with 62s in the British Open's second round. Bryson DeChambeau received a two-shot penalty for improving his swing path. Jon Rahm also received a conduct warning.

articles.headline: Venue concerns addressed for World Championships: Badminton India
articles.summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) confirmed that venue concerns, including poor air quality, cleanliness, and animal intrusions, raised during the India Open have been rectified for the upcoming World Championships in Delhi. Measures include new doors and improved facilities.

articles.headline: Arjun Erigaisi downs Niemann; Nihal Sarin holds Firouzja
articles.summary: Grandmaster Arjun Erigaisi secured his first win at the Chennai Grand Masters by outwitting Hans Niemann. Top-ranked Indian Alireza Firouzja, despite a draw with Nihal Sarin, continued to lead the table with 2.5 points.

articles.headline: Duke Of Tuscany fancied for main event at Bengaluru races
articles.summary: Duke Of Tuscany is expected to win the Governor’s Cup (2,400m), the chief event at the Bengaluru races on Sunday. The article also mentions the postponed fifth and sixth races from Saturday.

articles.headline: River Deep wins Dr. K.M. Srinivasa Gowda Memorial Cup
articles.summary: Mr. Md. Minhaj Raza’s River Deep, with A. Asbar up, won the Dr. K.M. Srinivasa Gowda Memorial Cup (1,200m), the main event at the Bengaluru races on Saturday. The Stewards postponed later races due to poor visibility and heavy rain.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank Profit Rises 5% Amid Margin Pressure
articles.summary: HDFC Bank reported a 5% increase in net profit to ₹19,059 crore for Q1, missing ETNow consensus estimates of 12-14%. Net interest income (NII) grew 1.7% to ₹29,088 crore. CASA ratio improved to 40.5% and deposits grew 18.3%.

articles.headline: Skyroot Aerospace Marks India Inc's Space Race 'Aagaman'
articles.summary: Skyroot Aerospace became the first private Indian company to place six payloads into orbit. Its Vikram-I rocket successfully deployed objects for ISRO, L&T, and educational institutes. This marks a new era for private participation in India's space sector.

articles.headline: ICICI Bank Net Profit Jumps 16% on Strong Loan Growth
articles.summary: ICICI Bank reported a 16% rise in Q1 net profit to ₹13,205 crore, driven by robust loan growth and steady margins. Net interest income (NII) grew 8.9% to ₹31,073 crore. Domestic loans rose 18.1%, with retail loans growing 17.5%.

articles.headline: Axis Bank Profit Up 23% on Steady NIMs, Lower Provisions
articles.summary: Axis Bank's Q1 standalone net profit increased 23% to ₹7,136 crore, beating estimates, supported by steady net interest margins (NIMs) and lower provisions. Net interest income (NII) rose 13% to ₹13,990 crore.

articles.headline: FM Sitharaman: India is the Place to do Business
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman urged entrepreneurs to 'shed hesitation' and 'take risks' to contribute to India's growth story. She emphasized creating a global environment, highlighting India's potential for innovation and growth.

articles.headline: DMDK & MNM File Response on Delimitation Case
articles.summary: The DMDK and MNM filed their responses in the Supreme Court regarding a petition challenging the use of the 2001 census for delimitation, arguing it penalizes states for population control. The case continues to attract responses from various parties.

articles.headline: Jana Sena Rejects Seat-sharing for Local Body Polls
articles.summary: The Jana Sena Party (JSP) rejected a seat-sharing formula with the TDP and BJP for local body elections, demanding equal treatment. The decision reflects internal party divisions and a desire for greater autonomy in alliance negotiations.

articles.headline: Quad Ministers May Meet in Manila Next Month
articles.summary: The Quad (India, US, Japan, Australia) foreign ministers are expected to meet in Manila next month. Discussions will focus on regional security, economic cooperation, and strategies to counter China's influence in the Indo-Pacific.

articles.headline: PDP, Lone's PC Not to Join Omar's Protest in Delhi
articles.summary: The PDP and People's Conference (PC) will not join Omar Abdullah's protest in Delhi against the J&K electoral process. They decided to skip the event, highlighting internal political disagreements among J&K parties.

articles.headline: Rijiju Invites Sudip for All-Party Meeting on Manipur
articles.summary: Kiren Rijiju invited Sudip Roy Barman, NCP's leader in Tripura, to an all-party meeting on Manipur. The meeting aims to discuss the ongoing ethnic violence and restore peace in the state.

articles.headline: Two More Arrested in Manipur Naga Killings Case
articles.summary: Joint team of NIA, Manipur Police, and CDDRF arrested two more individuals in connection with the June 11 Naga killings case. One suspect was arrested in Imphal, and another in Churachandpur. Investigations are ongoing.

articles.headline: India, Russia Eye Closer Cooperation on Drone Skills
articles.summary: India and Russia are exploring collaborations in drone technology and manufacturing. Discussions include joint production of drones and skill development programs, aiming to enhance indigenous capabilities and reduce reliance on imports.

articles.headline: Residents Protest Against Amazon Data Centre in Thane
articles.summary: Residents of Thane are protesting against the construction of an Amazon data center, citing environmental concerns and potential health risks. They demand proper impact assessments and adherence to regulations regarding water and waste management.

articles.headline: Abhishek Banerjee's Office Demolished in Kolkata
articles.summary: Kolkata civic authorities demolished the alleged illegal office of TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee following a directive from the Calcutta High Court. The structure was reportedly built without necessary permissions.

articles.headline: Navsari-Vapi-Raipur: Building Smart Township, One Step at a Time
articles.summary: The National Law University in Daman has acquired land in Navsari for a smart township project. The initiative aims to develop integrated residential, commercial, and educational zones, emphasizing sustainable urban planning and infrastructure development along the DNH corridor.

articles.headline: India Fights Social Impact with Abuse Norms for Solar Cells
articles.summary: India is pushing for a global consensus on human rights and ethical sourcing norms for solar cells. The initiative aims to prevent forced labor and other abuses in the supply chain, particularly from certain regions like Xinjiang, China.

articles.headline: IDBI Bank Posts Profit on High NII
articles.summary: IDBI Bank reported a net profit of ₹960 crore for Q1, reversing a loss from the previous year. This turnaround was driven by robust net interest income (NII) growth of 20% to ₹3,211 crore and improved asset quality.

articles.headline: AI Threatening Pyramid Model of Workforce, Reinventing Itself
articles.summary: Indian IT companies face challenges as AI disrupts the traditional pyramid model of their workforce. The article explores how companies must adapt, reskill employees, and leverage AI to remain competitive, with a focus on value creation rather than just cost efficiency.

articles.headline: Security Becomes Machine-Scale, Not Human-Scale
articles.summary: Cybersecurity is evolving from human-centric to machine-scale, driven by the vast number of devices and vulnerabilities. AI-powered security solutions are crucial for detecting threats and managing the complex digital landscape, emphasizing proactive defense over reactive measures.

articles.headline: Adults Collecting Everything from Funko Pop Figures, Lego
articles.summary: The article explores the growing trend of adults collecting toys like Funko Pop figures, Lego sets, and limited edition items. This phenomenon, driven by nostalgia and investment potential, creates a new consumer segment and market opportunities.

articles.headline: Vibe Check: Do Vibration Plate Workouts Make You Healthy?
articles.summary: The article examines the efficacy of vibration plate workouts for health and fitness. While some studies suggest benefits for muscle strength and bone density, experts recommend combining them with traditional exercise and caution against exaggerated claims.

articles.headline: Paris No Longer an Impossible Summit for Indian Fashion
articles.summary: Indian designers are increasingly showcasing their collections in Paris, challenging the traditional dominance of Western haute couture. The article highlights the growing global recognition of Indian aesthetics and the efforts to establish Paris as a key market.

articles.headline: If Boxed in Royalty at Wembley, Double Faults Likely
articles.summary: The article discusses the increasing presence of British royalty at Wimbledon, raising questions about its impact on the event's traditional character. It suggests that excessive royal involvement could distract from the sport itself.

articles.headline: Perfectly Prepared for Despair: Tactical Pessimism
articles.summary: The article explores "tactical pessimism" as a strategy for preparing for worst-case scenarios to mitigate potential setbacks. It discusses how embracing a realistic, even negative, outlook can lead to better planning and improved resilience in various life situations.

articles.headline: Jude & Erling Playing Footie, S-C-O-R-I-N-G!
articles.summary: The article discusses the friendship and on-field chemistry between footballers Jude Bellingham and Erling Haaland. It highlights their impressive performance and impact on their respective teams, making them key figures in modern football.

articles.headline: Queer Storytelling Leaking From Mainstream Language
articles.summary: The article observes how terms and narratives from queer subcultures are entering mainstream language, raising questions about appropriation versus broader acceptance. It discusses the impact of this linguistic shift on queer identity and representation.

articles.headline: The Importance of Testosterone Screening
articles.summary: The article discusses the increasing popularity of testosterone screening for men, driven by lifestyle factors and health awareness. It highlights the benefits of early detection for conditions related to hormone imbalance.

articles.headline: PM Modi: Youth's Role in Nation-Building Truly Heartening
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi praised Indian youth for their role in nation-building, innovation, and entrepreneurship. He highlighted their contributions across various sectors and expressed optimism about India's future, driven by the energy of its young population.

articles.headline: Iran Launches Fresh Attacks on Gulf, Jordan as Conflict Escalates
articles.summary: Iran reportedly launched drone and missile attacks targeting Gulf states and Jordan, escalating regional tensions. The US and its allies condemned the attacks, which targeted critical infrastructure and raised concerns about stability.

articles.headline: UN Court Orders Kundra to Pay $4.94m to EMV
articles.summary: A UN-backed court ordered Raj Kundra, Shilpa Shetty's husband, to pay $4.94 million to EMV Inc., a Dubai-based gold trading firm. The ruling pertains to an alleged gold import scam.

articles.headline: Trump Media Reports $100 Million Loss for Early Access
articles.summary: Trump Media & Technology Group reported a $100 million loss related to early access for Truth Social posts. The company faces financial challenges amidst efforts to stabilize its platform and attract more users.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank CEO Jagdishan's Term Ends in October
articles.summary: HDFC Bank CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan's term is set to end in October, prompting speculation about his successor. The bank's leadership transition is closely watched given its significant position in India's financial sector.

articles.headline: India's Demand For Working Capital Rises
articles.summary: The article highlights a significant increase in demand for working capital among Indian businesses. This surge is driven by economic growth, expanding operations, and the need for liquidity to manage operational expenses effectively.

articles.headline: Messi, Yamal's Connection Sets Stage for World Cup
articles.summary: The article explores the intergenerational connection between Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal, highlighting its significance for Argentina and Spain's World Cup prospects. Their shared heritage from La Masia symbolizes a blend of experience and emerging talent.

articles.headline: Messi Returns to Scene of His Lowest Ebb
articles.summary: Lionel Messi returned to Argentina to train with his national team, marking his first appearance since the Copa America final defeat. This return symbolizes a fresh start for Messi and the team as they prepare for upcoming tournaments.

articles.headline: Antonelli Takes Belgian GP Pole Position
articles.summary: Antonelli secured pole position at the Belgian Grand Prix, showcasing strong performance. This victory positions him favorably for the race, building momentum for his season.

articles.headline: FIFA Detects 7 Million Abusive Comments Online
articles.summary: FIFA detected over 7 million abusive comments targeting players during the World Cup, highlighting the growing issue of online harassment in sports. This prompts concerns about athlete well-being and the need for stronger social media regulations.

articles.headline: Dubey Replaces Injured Gundappa in Squad
articles.summary: Dubey has been called up as a replacement for the injured Gundappa, who sustained a shoulder injury during practice. This change affects team dynamics ahead of crucial matches.

articles.headline: Breaking Snooze: The Rise of Sleep Economy
articles.summary: The article explores the burgeoning "sleep economy," a market driven by products and services aimed at improving sleep quality. This includes smart beds, apps, supplements, and wellness retreats, reflecting a growing consumer awareness of sleep's importance.

articles.headline: Speaker Approves Six UBT MPs Merger With Shinde Sena
articles.summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla approved the merger of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs with the Eknath Shinde-led faction, following their request for separate seating for the monsoon session. This decision reduces Sena (UBT)'s strength to three and increases Shinde's to 13. The Speaker also approved separate seating for 20 rebel Trinamool Congress MPs.

articles.headline: India's First Private Orbital Rocket Vikram-1 Successfully Launched
articles.summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1, India's first private orbital rocket, from Satish Dhawan Space Centre. It placed four technology demonstration payloads and postcards into low earth orbit at 450km altitude. This milestone makes India the third country globally to achieve private orbital launch capability, hailed by PM Modi as a "defining moment" and "grand success."

articles.headline: Nine Dead in Illegal Gujarat Firecracker Factory Blaze
articles.summary: A massive fire and blast at an illegal firecracker manufacturing unit in Ahmedabad, Gujarat, killed nine people and injured seven. Police booked three individuals for operating without a valid license, and a case of culpable homicide will be registered. Prime Minister Modi and Chief Minister Patel announced ex-gratia relief of ₹2 lakh for the deceased families.

articles.headline: Wangchuk Hospitalized on Day 21 of Hunger Strike, Opposition Condemns Move
articles.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly moved by Delhi Police from Jantar Mantar to Safdarjung Hospital on the 21st day of his hunger strike. Protesting for Ladakh's constitutional rights and alleged exam irregularities, he reportedly refused medical interventions and sought the Education Minister's resignation. His wife was denied access to his physician and lawyer. The move, related to a Delhi High Court order, was condemned by opposition leaders.

articles.headline: Punjab Police Inspector Indicted by FBI for Extortion Scheme
articles.summary: Punjab Police Inspector Gurinderjit Singh Nagra was arrested after being indicted by the US Federal Bureau of Investigation for an extortion conspiracy. Nagra is accused of attempting to extort $400,000 from a US-based family by threatening fabricated criminal charges in India and is linked to an India-based organised crime syndicate. He has been remanded to police custody in Hoshiarpur.

articles.headline: 'Article 370' Wins Best Film at 72nd National Film Awards
articles.summary: The 72nd National Film Awards honored 'Article 370' as Best Feature Film and Yami Gautam as Best Actress for her role in it. Kartik Aaryan (Chandu Champion) and Mammootty (Bramayugam) shared the Best Actor award, marking Mammootty's fourth win. These prestigious awards recognize excellence in Indian cinema for the year.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu to Observe July 18, November 1 as State Formation Days
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin announced that July 18 (renaming resolution) and November 1 (erstwhile Madras State formation) will both be officially observed as State Formation Days. This decision reverses a previous DMK government choice to celebrate only July 18, aiming to honor the state's linguistic history with organized cultural events.

articles.headline: Abhishek Banerjee Challenges TMC Rebels to Resign and Return
articles.summary: TMC National General Secretary Abhishek Banerjee challenged rebel party leaders who joined BJP or blamed him for the party's downfall, daring them to resign from their posts before seeking to return. He vowed to resign if they returned to Mamata Banerjee, accusing them of aligning with BJP for agency protection. He emphasized that those who "abused" the party during elections would face strict scrutiny.

articles.headline: Amit Shah Reviews Border Security, Unveils BSF Projects in Bengal
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah reviewed security along the Indo-Bangladesh border in West Bengal's Siliguri corridor, described as "once a corridor of infiltration," during his three-day visit. He inaugurated three BSF projects, including advanced surveillance systems and new border outposts, and laid foundation stones for projects worth ₹77 crore. Shah emphasized a zero-tolerance policy against cross-border infiltration and illegal activities, stressing indigenous modern technology.

articles.headline: CBSE Class 10 Second Exam Results Show Score Improvement
articles.summary: CBSE declared Class 10 Second Board Exam results, with an overall pass percentage of 96.78%. A significant 59.95% of students who appeared improved their scores compared to the main exam held from May 15-21. This special examination allows candidates to boost performance in previously cleared subjects, leading to a reported rise in overall pass percentages and high scores.

articles.headline: Spain, Argentina Set For Historic FIFA World Cup Final Showdown
articles.summary: Spain and Argentina are set to clash in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final at New Jersey’s MetLife Stadium, marking their first-ever World Cup final encounter. The match is billed as a "final dance" for 39-year-old Lionel Messi and a platform for 19-year-old Lamine Yamal. The final also sees Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni face his former instructor, Spain head coach Luis de La Fuente.

articles.headline: PV Sindhu Enters First Tournament Final in Two Years at Japan Open
articles.summary: Double Olympic medalist PV Sindhu has reached the final of the Japan Open badminton, marking her first tournament final in over two years. She secured her spot after World No. 4 Chen Yufei retired midway due to a hamstring injury. This significant comeback for Sindhu follows a series of injuries and a dip in form. She will face Japan's Akane Yamaguchi in the final.

articles.headline: HDFC, ICICI Banks Report Strong Q1 Net Profit Growth
articles.summary: Major Indian banks reported strong Q1 FY25 results. HDFC Bank's standalone net profit climbed 5% to ₹19,060 crore, driven by a 6.7% NII rise to ₹33,536 crore (NIM 3.26%), with CEO Jagdishan focusing on credit-deposit ratio improvement. ICICI Bank's net profit surged 16% to ₹14,805 crore, fueled by robust lending and lower provisions, despite slight NIM moderation.

articles.headline: FM Sitharaman Urges Young Entrepreneurs to Build Boldly
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman urged young entrepreneurs to be "bold," take risks, and build globally competitive businesses. Speaking at a summit, she emphasized innovation and their crucial contribution to India's growth story. Sitharaman highlighted the robust startup ecosystem, government support, and a favorable business environment, stressing the need to navigate current global geopolitical and economic disruptions.

articles.headline: PDP Sets Terms for Joining NC Protest in Delhi
articles.summary: The PDP indicated it may skip the NC-led protest for J&K Statehood in Delhi. PDP president Mehbooba Mufti wrote to Farooq Abdullah, stating her party's participation depends on the NC including demands for Article 370 restoration, release of political prisoners, and lifting of the Jamaat-e-Islami ban in its agenda.

articles.headline: SC Halts Demolition of Dargahs, Homes in Rajasthan Border Areas
articles.summary: The Supreme Court granted interim relief, halting the demolition of mosques, madrasas, dargahs, and homes in Rajasthan's border districts for two weeks. The court directed petitioners, represented by Jamiat Ulama-i-Hind, to seek further relief from the Rajasthan High Court at Jodhpur.

articles.headline: Banking Sector Q1 Profits Surge: Kotak 26%, ICICI 16%, Axis 23% Growth
articles.summary: Major Indian banks reported strong Q1 profit growth. Kotak Mahindra Bank's net profit climbed 26% to ₹4,728 crore, driven by robust loan growth and improved asset quality (NIM 4.53%). ICICI Bank saw a 16% rise in net profit to ₹14,805 crore (NIM 4.36%), with NII up 12.7%. Axis Bank's net profit grew 23% to ₹7,114 crore (NIM 3.46%), with NII rising 8%.

articles.headline: Yes Bank Q1 Net Profit Climbs 33.4% on Higher Interest Income
articles.summary: Yes Bank reported a 33.4% increase in net profit to ₹1,071 crore for the quarter ended June 30. This significant growth was driven by a 17.5% increase in net interest income to ₹2,786.4 crore, along with strong loan growth and improved asset quality. Gross non-profitable assets were notably reduced to 1.3%.

articles.headline: Rohit Sharma's Future, Series on Line in Lord's ODI Decider
articles.summary: The third ODI between England and India at Lord's is a standalone series decider, with all eyes on Rohit Sharma. Speculation about his ODI future and recent patchy form overshadows the match, where his performance is critical for India's success. Fans and critics are closely watching his leadership and batting. KL Rahul is expected to play for India after illness.
